item.content:encoded: Shit was this good. Wonderfully written and engaging - what more can I want?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very late response, but ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

When I wrote that final iteration, I intentionally obfuscated what happened even to myself to add that that level of confusion. I feel it works better that way, to both highlight the level of distrust that someone in that situation would feel, because suddenly no one is safe, but also to reinforce the separation between the O5s and the rest of staff.

In this SCP, the O5s are absolutely the villains, so I wanted to show how they weren't trustworthy either because they were infected themselves, or that they're so paranoid that they think everyone else is infected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm confused. Is O5-1 immune or just contaminated and thought to be immune.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So at the end basically everyone is infected, and life is carried on normally with everyone infected
+1, that was great

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved the part at the end where the Foundation proposes different ways of containing this SCP. You can see how the O5 votes for and against each proposal get smaller and smaller with each one until the last one's just a 1-1 vote, implying the Foundation's almost completely under her control and/or the O5 council members went into hiding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love your entry, especially how it built up from a small harmless anomaly to the best infohazard I have ever read. But one thing Iwished you would have done was to provide a little more examples for assasination attempts and a backstory for Project Lethe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that does seem to be the implication, doesn't it? lily is terrifying for the foundation, but she doesn't really seem to wish to harm those who haven't harmed her. otherwise, with (iirc) 83% of the population affected by her, wouldn't the world be in way deeper shit if her intent was truly to cause a global MK scenario? it seems to me the foundation is in danger but not necessarily the rest of the populace, especially since she allows people infected by her to "act and live normally." I feel like if her intent was to destroy all humanity she could basically have accomplished that by now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was one of my favorite articles long before I joined the site, and it's always thrilling and horrifying on a re-read. Thank you for this.

One question: the world doesn't really end as the Foundation claims, does it? Sure, "the person effectively loses conscious control of their bodies," but "Generally, SCP-3002 allows her victims to live and act normally…"

Even though it sounds like Lily's trying to kill the entire Foundation (I would, given what they put her through), once she finishes with that and everybody remembers her, would humanity return to some altered form of normalcy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Me too, was absolutely loving this until that arbitrary figure.
Not really necessary to execute people or have such a multi-layered containment procedure if nearly everyone has it already.

item.title: Re: Bad Humour
item.content:encoded: That joke was made within days of me posting this. Just not in the discussion page.

item.title: Bad Humour
item.content:encoded:
I'm surprised nobody has made this joke yet

Begone thought

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only thing I want to address here is to question if calling it a memetic hazard is correct, at least once they understand what it really is.

As far as I understand, memetic anomalies are, ultimately, non-anomalous stimulus that trigger anomalous response. In a sense, the anomaly already exist as *potentia* in the people affected. It spreads in the sense that people affected are usually prone to reproduce the trigger, or otherwise the occurrence of the trigger itself indicates a high probability of new occurrences.

Iris, in this skip, is a sentient thought that is spreading. She isn't neither bound to a given trigger, nor pre-existent on affected subjects. She is an infohazard, not meme.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey any chance we could get the r-slur removed/redacted in this? I'd proposed changing the statement for something more euphemistic with the same intent (e.g, "she didn't seem uh, all there")

item.title: It really should have been a story.
item.content:encoded: I'll start off saying that this is a good SCP, but it is more structured as a story. The build up is amazing, starting off as what seemed like a harmless memetic memory that evolved into basically the fall of the foundation. The response of the GOIs were great, the proposals of what to do were great. My big issue is that it doesn't fit in well with the whole foundation stuff, which can be fixed with some edits. First main issue, why was the D-class patient zero? It states that amnestics works to counter the anomaly before a certain point so was the D-class not given them? I understand if you don't like a canon where D-class are terminated at the end of the month, but if the foundation doesn't amnesticize the D-class at least then the foundation in the canon isn't really the foundation at that point. Same with the termination of SCP 105, I understand that she was quite mentally broken from project Lethe, but termination is just against what the foundation stands for because it's in the name. The reason that the foundation and the GOC don't get along is on this fundamental difference, the foundation does not terminate SCPs unless it needs to for basically this reason, remember 1609? These issues can be solved with some editing, I do love how the foundation in an attempt to solve future potential issues instead made another much larger issue. I don't like to think that this article exists as a "kick the Foundation's teeth in" type of thing since that can really cause an article to lose depth, because that tells more about the writer rather than tell an idea or a story. I will say that this story has a "evil lab tortures a girl with special powers and now she's back for revenge" type thing, especially with her torture, but I'm pretty fine with it as is, especially the suicide part. It really should have been a story to be fair, with it's use of an already existing SCP and the 5 minutes till the fall of the foundation stuff. I find it to be similar to "The Stars Do Not Wait For You" with it's use of a past SCP to show the end of the world. I don't have a lot to say other than that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very late response, but both Athenadora and Antigerone are correct in their interpretation of Lily as Iris, even down to the naming conventions. And while I didn't have Rainbow Body in mind, specifically, it certainly does work, doesn't it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was probably pointed out by someone else but is the "Restart" proposal related to the ending of SCP 2998 and Roget's 001 proposal? If so, that would explain a few things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it weird that I'm spooked b/c Jan. 17th is my birthday?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't forget

+ Spoiler
- Hide spoiler

Iris, Greek goddess of the rainbow

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was awesome!

+ show block
– hide block

When I first read this I got kind of confused at the GOI list you gave us. At first I had thought you are trying to say that Lily had subverted every other GOI. It's a pet peave of mine that I don't like it when people try to make their skip something that can beat everybody (even 2935 bugged me in that respect and that's one of my favorite skips). But then I read the comments and I realized that you were actually saying the opposite, that it's ambiguos what other GOIs are doing. (I think anyone who gave no response back is pronably safe. Since Nobody is one person and his message didn't have Lily in it I'd say that's proof he's okay for now).

So yeah, this was good. While their are plenty of articles that try to be scary, this is one of the few that legitimately creaped me out. So, if I'm correct, I don't think Lily directally controls anyone. I think what she does is she has complete control over memories. So if she wanted you to walk down a hallway, she wouldn't force you to walk down it. She'd change yoyr memories so you'd remember you wanted to go down there and. So you're technically still in control, but Lily can completely change your personality, beliefs, goals and… Ugh. That is so much more horrifying than classic mind control.

So anyway, how are the GOIs going to stop her? Well, we know that Nobody at least is okay. But wait, isn't there someone else who should be able immune to her powers? Someone who has survived everything from randomized physics to the plot being rewritten to attacks from beings capable of killing him? That's right, it's obvious how this is going to go down. Nobody is going to hollow out 682 and use him like a mech suit to run around the world and destroy all Lily infected individuals and information! Clearly this is the most sane and reasonable solution. A gecko mech!

+1 Thanks for writing this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A beautiful death.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is this, a crossover episode?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, "Veselka" is Ukrainian for "rainbow". Does that make "Lily Veselka"

+ REDACTED
- REDACTED

Iris Thompson after being subjected to Project RAINBOW BODY, losing most of her memory, and only vaguely recalling that her first name was a flower and the protocol used on her included the word 'rainbow' (presumably spoken aloud in Ukrainian since that's where the site was located)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: People being terminate for having the same thing as 78% of the world population doesn't make sense. The whole infection thing is pretty weird when you put this in, are they going to test and kill all of their staff? evidently they didn't, but apparently discovering infected by talking to people is enough for killing them. -1, took me right out of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably for the same reason they didn't go for SCP-2000. It is never said where the last few personnel are stationed, so they could be pretty far from SCP-1968.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, that part was actually one of the parts that I feel a lot of people haven't picked up on, because to me, it's the most terrifying part, which is why I threw it in there.

If she can affect your muscle memory, she can do things like make you forget how to drive while on the highway. Make you forget how to walk when hiking. Make you forget the route to your home, so you just wander endlessly. It's a terrifying prospect for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't sure how I felt about this until I got to the part where it said she affected implicit memories, and I let out a verbal "OH SHIT!" And it only got better from there.

"His name is also John. I'm not calling myself my own best friend. it's a separate human being."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this, even though it left me wanting more over and over again.

Enthusiastic +1.

Also, my only addition to this site is 1139 The Broken Tongue. It occurs to me that throwing a lot of electricity through that would potentially fix the problem…it would create a brand NEW problem, and at best would quarantine her in each person's mind, but an interesting thought.

Or can she travel through body language?

It's been just over a year since this was posted and I've just crested this monumental milestone. I am still so thrilled this SCP has been received as well as it has been. Thank you all for reading this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What if O5-1 was the one who made 3002 and they are using it to purge the foundation and seize control of the foundation? This is backed up by the fact O5-1 is called Veles and the person who the letter in iteration 3 was addressed to was also called Veles, and the unusual step of terminating people affected by 3002.

P.S. another thing is that the Project Lethe was an undocumented site, meaning that it could have been rogue.

item.title: A great article
item.content:encoded: A great article, the techniques of writting shocked me. When you follow this article and get to know the truth one step by one step, the feeling are crazily wonderful. You never know what the truth is when facing this crazy item.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If things have already reached the point where 2000 is being discussed, why not just deploy 1968? It seems like a pretty perfect counter given on how this SCP's creation was dependent on a single historical event which could easily be butterflied out of existence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry for the somewhat slow reply. I didn't want to label this as Apollyon, even if it seems fitting, because I wanted to give a sense that there was a chance it could be fixed, which is also why I have the rather cryptic "RESTART" on the final line. I wanted people to try to come to their own conclusion as to whether or not they managed to "save the world" in this case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Feels like this is more Apollyon SCP rather than Keter.

item.title: Ará Orún?
item.content:encoded: Hey, what about MTF ω-0 ("Ará Orún")? Since they are immune to memetic agents, shouldn't they have been able to help fighting her?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I… could imagine SCP-3002 not killing anyone after taking over the world like this. She could control all of humanity from there and steer them. Is that possible for her?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoy the theme of karmic justice in this article. Obviously the Foundation's punishment for what they did to *Lily was disproportionate, but this is one of the few world-ending SCPs that doesn't make me sympathize with them at all. Well done..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a genuinely interesting and unique concept and I hope to see more of your work in the future.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An entity that is truly and completely intangible, yet should she desire she can harm you in indirect ways. Best stay on her good side…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The eeriest thing about this is how you can see the deterioration of the SCP Foundation in the last iteration. The opposing votes for each of the proposed actions progressively become lower and lower numbers as time goes on, and at the end, there are only 2, which implies that all other O5s had been contaminated and terminated.

EDIT: I just realized this has been said before. Nevertheless, it's an awesomely creepy touch.

This just crested +400, so have some fanart that was literally just posted by Zhange.

+ show block
– hide block

https://zhange-art.tumblr.com/ <- Does dope art/is cool

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hell yeah it is. No wonder she was trying to wipe everyone out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hell, what makes this better is that she can manipulate specific parts of set information, or photographs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fucking hell, what did they do to her?

To exist only as a memory is a sad and scary concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree. I don't get why people are having their jimmies rustled that a person from 2006 said something that is taboo 11 years afterward. The interview takes place in 2006, not 2017.

Either way the usage of "retarded" felt perfectly natural, if not with some needlessly tacked-on negative connotations, in such a context. A mentally perfect child does not do something like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I saw the lack of responses as more of a "but we're not close allies, what's to say you won't sabotage our agents?" than a "what if the foundation is unintentionally trying to infect us with scp-3002?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, shit. It has the O5 Council.

Also partway through the final document, when it shifted from referring to the anomaly as "it" to "she?" Yeah, that's when I upvoted. This was freaking brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, I was planning to respond but after that I my response would just look bad in comparison.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that the format of how we're getting this kind of implies that this could be from before 2000 was used, and the real SCP is this document itself, most likely spreading her from beyond the grave of the old world… At least, that's my new headcanon. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with everything you said until the last paragraph. As a result, I voted -1. Not that it matters because it does seem like a LOT of people like to see the Foundation fail. I guess it's just boredom with the first thousand or something similar.

I liked the first page a lot, and I like to imagine how it's possible something could destroy the world, but I don't like being told it's basically already happened and the Foundation is pointless. This seems to be the ultimate example of one of the many, many SCPs that has destroyed the world (5 minutes after the article ends.)
Now, if this was harmless, if people did not stop caring about their own lives and safety when infected, I'd be completely okay with 78(I believe that was the number)% infection, because that's scary but not world ending.
This couldn't happen, because the world ends, and the world isn't over. It completely destroys my suspension of disbelief, while also making the Foundation, once again, a pointless organisation.
I think we only should have had a couple of SCPs like this, though I'm okay with tales.

Anyway, so there's my downvote. Obviously this is what people like and therefore there will probably be many more of these, the writing was good, I liked the first iteration or two, but the overall effect of the SCP was just not for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I never said it was deadly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aside from being a "Deadly info-spreading thing", this is probably the most terrifying 3000. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, the Project Lethe mentioned in 1425 isn't the same one here. But that's not stopping you from thinking they are. They way it's implied to work in 1425 and the way I imply Lethe works in this SCP are somewhat similar, so it's possible it's the same procedure. It did come up when I was writing this that a Project Lethe existed already, and I've adopted that into my own headcanon. Since this Project Lethe was an off-the-books project, it may make that the O5 command used the name of a pre-existing project to further mask what they were doing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Out of curiosity…

Is the Project Lethe here the same Project LETHE mentioned in 1425?

(Also, of course, +1. The email format is a wonderful framing device for this, giving it a great sense of progression, and the individual iterations themselves had me engaged all the way through to the last line.)

item.title: Holy. Fucking. Shit.
item.content:encoded:
There isn't much I can say that hasn't already been said. But I'm gonna give it my damn best to try.

This is bloody glorious. Just everything about it. I can't phrase just how freaking amazing this is. +1, then -1 so I could give it another +1…times 100, to the power of infinity. This might be one of my favourite things on this damn site.

I'm not exactly the smartest person in the world, so I didn't notice that the Foundation had created this thing. But even without that knowledge, this was bloody amazing!

Please, PLEASE make a tale where the last two uninfected people (O5s) go on a ragtag journey across the world to find a happy ending, with romance, drama, action, suspense! Or don't. Would still be awesome without one.

In my opinion, this should have came first in the contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Still no, eventually, she will be in all information created from when she began spreading onwards. We make a ton of information every day. This is information. I created it right now. It would be extremely difficult to live life without seeing any information, past a certain date.

Translating it is not going to change the fact that she is that information to begin with. In 2979, redacting or translating may work because the effect is not the word itself. It happens when the word is perceived.

3002 is different. She is the information itself. If you were to translate "Lily Veselka" into spanish. It would still be "Lily Veselka" but in the spanish language, the original information is still there, it's just been altered slightly. If you look at a painting through blue tinted glasses, you're still looking at the painting. This is the same concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I though she had to create the information for it to be infecting? If the information someone receives is not what she made, how would she get across?
I mean, if you took 2979's actual name, and put it through a translator(somehow), i imagine it would no longer have the effect. That sort of thing, but on a larger scale, is what i was thinking of.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is my nomination for best comment of 2017.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is exactly what I intended. I'm glad you picked up on that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Slow decrease in O5 votes was subtle and awesome. Didn't even realise it was happening until I got to the last one and saw 1-1 and thought "Wait, that's not right".

Anyways, I don't feel as horrified as some other people do. From what I can tell, you maintain your personality unless she needs to use you, and she's only hostile to certain Foundation personnel. In the end, everyone will be infected, and she will be effectively a god, but she doesn't seem like a necessarily malevolent god. She seems like a curious god who wants to get back at people who've done terrible shit to her, but after that's done, maybe she'll just be ever present and all knowing.

She might even be protecting.

It's wrong that I find this sort of comforting isn't it…

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: do you mean thought?
